# Story Outline Template

Use this template to outline a new story. Copy the whole `_story_template/` folder to a new directory named `##. [Story Title]` and fill in the sections below, then expand into the chapter-by-chapter breakdown in `../chapters/00_story_outline.md`.

---

## Story Premise

**Title:** [Working title]
**Central problem:** [One-sentence description]
**Stakes:** [What's at risk? Calibrate to your tone ceiling — [ai_instructions.md](../../../ai_instructions.md) §7]
**Antagonist:** [Who or what is behind it?]

---

## Character Focus

**POV Character:** [Who anchors the narration?]
**Character with most at stake:** [Whose problem is this?]
**Character arc emphasis:** [Which character grows most in this story?]

---

## Anchor Element Plan

*(If your universe uses a recurring anchor element — see [ai_instructions.md](../../../ai_instructions.md) §9 — plan its appearances before writing. Lock it early and reference it consistently across chapters.)*

| Part | Emotional State | How the Anchor Reflects It |
|------|-----------------|----------------------------|
| **Part 1** | [e.g., comfort, routine] | |
| **Part 2** | [e.g., excitement, discovery] | |
| **Part 3** | [e.g., crisis, defeat] | |
| **Part 4** | [e.g., resolution] | |

---

## Four-Part Structure

*(Rename/renumber the parts to your structure; keep the beat tables. Beat names below follow Save the Cat as a worked example — substitute your own framework.)*

### Part 1: Setup

| Beat | What Happens |
|------|--------------|
| Opening Image | |
| Theme Stated | |
| Set-up | |
| Catalyst | |
| Debate | |

**Foundation plants:**
1.
2.

### Part 2: Development

| Beat | What Happens |
|------|--------------|
| B Story | |
| Fun and Games | |
| Midpoint | |

**Misdirection (if a mystery — one red-herring theory per character is a cheap way to characterize while misdirecting):**
-

**Connective plant:**

### Part 3: Crisis

| Beat | What Happens |
|------|--------------|
| Bad Guys Close In | |
| All Is Lost | |
| Dark Night of the Soul | |
| Break into Three | |

**Confirmation plant:**

### Part 4: Resolution

| Beat | What Happens |
|------|--------------|
| Finale | |
| Final Image | |

**Keystone plant / reveal:**

---

## Solution Grid

*(For mysteries and any story with a hidden truth.)*

| Element | Details |
|---------|---------|
| What really happened | |
| Who is responsible | |
| Why they did it | |
| How it's discovered | |
| How it's resolved | |

**Fair play check:** Can the reader work it out from planted material? [ ] Yes [ ] No (fix it)

---

## Canon Updates Needed

After completing this story, update the following. Log all changes in [CANON_CHANGELOG.md](../../../CANON_CHANGELOG.md).

### Mandatory
- [ ] **Character lore tracking** — Add a "Book N" section to each main character's canon file with key events, quotes, and growth
- [ ] **Locations** — Add any new locations; update existing entries if details changed
- [ ] **Glossary** — Add new terms, recurring elements, and canon rules revealed in this story
- [ ] **Continuity summary** — Update `canon library/continuity.md` with world-state changes

### If Applicable
- [ ] **Group dynamics** — Update if relationships shifted
- [ ] **Antagonist file** — Fill in or update the antagonist profile for this book
- [ ] **Series plan** — Note deviations from the planned outline, or seeds planted for future books
- [ ] **Story-specific character files** — Promote any minor characters who should recur to the canon library
